hero.title: Personal Overseas Remittance
hero.subtitle: VPAY is a personal cross-border remittance service designed to help users send money from Korea to overseas recipients with more speed and clarity. Users can review applied FX rates and fees before sending and track transaction status in the app.

items.quote: With VPAY’s low fees and rapid delivery, sending love to my family back home has become much more effortless.
I can send living expenses to my child abroad with total peace of mind, thanks to VPAY's transparent exchange rates and real-time tracking.
VPAY handles bulk transfers and settlements so reliably that our complex international business transactions have become incredibly seamless.

items.title: Pooling
items.description:
Technology that bundles multiple remittances and processes them at once
Reduces intermediary costs, increases processing speed, lowers user fees, and is especially effective for bulk B2B transfers

items.title: Pre-funding
items.description:
Technology that pays out instantly using funds pre-deposited in the recipient country
Enables fast transfer speed, lowers failure rates, and works well for B2B payouts such as payroll

items.title: Smart Routing
items.description:
Technology that automatically selects the fastest, cheapest, and most reliable route
Factors include speed, cost, success rate, partner status, and regulations / time zones

items.question: Is only person-to-person transfer available?
items.answer: The current main service presentation focuses on personal remittance, while business transfer information is provided on a separate page.

items.question: How much is the fee?
items.answer: It depends on destination and amount. Users can check the estimated fee and applied FX rate before sending.

items.question: What is the transfer limit?
items.answer: Limits apply according to applicable regulations and service policy. Please refer to the Fees / Limits page.

items.question: How do I check the transfer status?
items.answer: Users can check the current status in the transaction history inside the app.

items.question: How long does it take?
items.answer: Most transfers are processed quickly, but timing may vary by country, bank, and time window.

hero.title: Business Payment / Transfer
hero.subtitle: VPAY is preparing business payment and transfer support to help companies manage cross-border fund movement more clearly, including supplier payments, partner settlement, and operational transfers.

reviews.title: Target Reviews
reviews.quote: Sending payments to overseas suppliers used to be a hassle, but since automating with VPAY’s API, our operational burden has significantly decreased, and we can now handle international payments quickly and reliably.
reviews.author: Small Business & E-commerce

items.question: Is this only for businesses?
items.answer: Yes. This page is for business payment / transfer introduction. Personal remittance is explained separately on the personal transfer page.

items.question: Is API integration available immediately?
items.answer: This is currently an introduction-stage page. Actual integration scope and schedule will be discussed separately.

items.question: What type of businesses is this for?
items.answer: Suitable for companies that pay overseas suppliers, settle with partners, or handle recurring cross-border transfers.

items.question: How is pricing structured?
items.answer: Pricing may vary depending on flow and destination. Detailed conditions should be discussed separately.

hero.title: FX Guidance
hero.subtitle: VPAY provides transparent FX guidance so users can review applied exchange rates and fee structure before submitting a transfer.

items.quote: Every time I imported coffee beans, checking rates and sending payments took too long. With V Pay's FX module, the amount is calculated automatically and processed instantly, which has greatly reduced the burden of running the business.
When I receive revenue from overseas platforms, exchange-rate swings often eat into my earnings. With V Pay's real-time FX, the best rate is applied so I can settle my income more steadily.

items.title: Multi-Currency Core Engine
items.description: Accurately manages balances and currencies across multiple countries.

items.title: Real-Time Rate Module
items.description: Reflects market movements instantly to provide fair exchange pricing.

items.title: Global Partner Network (API Integration)
items.description: Connects with local payout rails to support fast and stable FX execution.

items.title: Automated Settlement System
items.description: Automates exchange and settlement so even large, multi-country transactions are handled quickly and reliably.

items.question: Is the FX rate always fixed in real time?
items.answer: Applied FX may vary depending on service policy and operational conditions. The confirmed conditions shown before transfer are the key reference.

items.question: Are fees separate from FX?
items.answer: Yes. Fees may be applied separately depending on destination and transfer conditions.

items.question: Where can I check the FX rate?
items.answer: Users can review it in the FX guidance screen and again during the transfer flow.

items.quote: Designed for users who need to send living expenses back home quickly and simply.

values.title: Core Brand Values

As a South Korean fintech company, V Pay expands its global payment network through strategic partnerships and regulatory cooperation, focusing on Southeast Asia.
Leveraging stable Korean technology and experience, it provides locally tailored digital payment solutions and pursues sustainable growth and increased market share.

detail.title: 12cm — Smart Label Solution Developer for Fintech Platforms

sections.heading: An Electronic Stamp Solution Designed for Global Markets
paragraphs: Today, we introduce 12cm, a platform development company for fintech businesses. Vice President Shin Sung-won shares the company story.

As an IT startup, 12cm has been driving domestic and international projects through Echoss, an innovative technology platform that applies a point-earning system using the smart stamp service Echoss Stamp on smartphones. With it, users can activate and authenticate loyalty services through simple actions on their phones.

sections.heading: Simple Payments Through Electronic Stamps on Smartphones
paragraphs: Director Han Jeong-gyu and Vice President Shin Sung-won of 12cm are fintech specialists with experience in mobile financial systems such as online banking, and became interested in services that connect online and offline experiences while delivering banking solutions.

To address this, 12cm built a smart stamp system. Working through the smartphone's capacitive sensor, Echoss Stamp combines with cloud-based management software to complete authentication, offering a more affordable and convenient alternative to barcode or QR-based systems.

sections.heading: Echoss Stamp Is Already Used by 50 Domestic Companies Across 24 Countries
paragraphs: At cafes and bakeries, customers often receive loyalty cards that are stamped with each purchase. Because paper stamp cards can be inconvenient, 12cm created a digital equivalent that lets users attach electronic stamps to their smartphones.

The solution is now used by 50 Korean companies including Hyundai Department Store and Baskin Robbins, and it has expanded to 24 countries including Japan, China, Taiwan, Singapore, Spain, and the United States.

sections.heading: From O2O to Big Data and AI, Expanding Services Further
paragraphs: All required software is delivered through a cloud environment, while the stamp remains the only offline device. Compared with other payment systems, the smart stamp platform is both more convenient and more cost-efficient.

Built on years of accumulated data, 12cm is expanding into big data by creating new services based on data analysis, extraction, and integration. The company is also researching how to combine that foundation with AI technology to forecast trends.

sections.heading: Aiming to Grow Into a Unicorn in Technology
paragraphs: 12cm is among the first companies in Korea to launch cloud-based financial services, aiming to set new standards in a sector where strong security requirements have traditionally made innovation difficult.
The company plans to keep expanding with a focus on innovation across O2O services, big data, and AI, while also pursuing socially meaningful growth from an ESG perspective.

policyPage.metaTitle: Payment and Security Policy | V Pay
policyPage.metaDescription: V Pay policy page covering payment statuses, security, and the transparency of transaction processing.
policyPage.heroSubtitle: This page explains identity verification, security, transaction status, and cancellation / refund guidance users should know when using VPAY.

complianceSections.title: Regulations and Licenses
complianceSections.description:
V Pay strictly complies with both global and domestic financial regulations to provide a stable and trustworthy service.
We secure the licenses and certifications required across multiple countries and markets, enabling business expansion while protecting users.
bullets: Compliance with domestic financial regulations: full adherence to the Foreign Exchange Transactions Act, Electronic Financial Transactions Act, and related laws
Global licensing secured: acquisition of key country-specific approvals for remittance, payments, and digital asset services
KYC / AML policy: automated systems for customer identity verification and anti-money laundering controls
Data protection and security: compliance with personal data protection laws and international standards such as ISO / IEC 27001
Response to regulatory changes: flexible adaptation to new laws and policy changes to minimize business risk

complianceSections.title: Security
complianceSections.description: Security architecture (2FA, Transaction Risk Engine, and more)
bullets: 2FA multi-factor authentication to prevent account takeover
Transaction Risk Engine analyzes transaction patterns and detects abnormal transactions in real time
Data encryption, access control, and log monitoring
Data encryption, access control, and log monitoring
High availability, real-time response, and regulatory compliance

reasons.title: Why Fees Can Be More Efficient

items.title: Minimized intermediary costs
items.description: We connect directly with global partners instead of routing through multiple financial intermediaries, removing unnecessary fees.

items.title: Fully digital operations
items.description: Automated remittance, payment, and FX processes reduce operational overhead.

items.title: Economies of scale
items.description: Pooling large funds together minimizes the unit cost of each transaction.

items.title: High-performance FX engine and real-time rates
items.description: Optimal path analysis and real-time exchange rates reduce conversion costs.

items.title: Optimized regulation and licensing
items.description: We strategically secure global financial licenses and regulatory coverage to reduce legal costs.

items.title: Smart routing technology
items.description: Automatic route optimization saves both cost and processing time.

items.title: Global partner network
items.description:
We integrate directly with local partners such as Wise, Tranglo, and GME to secure stability and cost efficiency.
*Technology and operational efficiency make global payments faster, safer, and more affordable.
